verses.text: The LORD to Moses and Aaron: take the sum of the sons of Kohath from among the Levites — from thirty years to fifty, all that enter into the service for work in the tabernacle. Guidance: A second Levite census — this time of those fit for active duty. Thirty to fifty: the years of full strength. God's service isn't for the youngest or the oldest, but for those whose bodies can lift what holiness weighs.

The LORD to Moses and Aaron: take the sum of the sons of Kohath from among the Levites — from thirty years to fifty, all that enter into the service for work in the tabernacle. The LORD to Moses and Aaron: take the sum of the sons of Kohath from among the Levites — from thirty years to fifty, all that enter into the service for work in the tabernacle. Guidance: A second Levite census — this time of those fit for active duty. Thirty to fifty: the years of full strength. God's service isn't for the youngest or the oldest, but for those whose bodies can lift what holiness weighs.

Kohath's service — the most holy things. When camp moves, Aaron and sons take down the vail, cover the ark, table, candlestick, gold altar, vessels, brass altar. Wrap with cloth and badgers' skins. After covering, the Kohathites bear it. They shall not touch any holy thing, lest die. Guidance: The priests pack the holy things; the Kohathites only carry. Hands that bear are not the hands that touch. God protects His holiness even from those closest to it — the wrapping is mercy.

Eleazar son of Aaron has charge of the oil, sweet incense, daily meat offering, anointing oil, oversight of the tabernacle. Cut not off Kohath — appoint each man to his service. They shall not go in to see when the holy things are covered, lest they die. Guidance: Even looking too soon could kill. Aaron covers first; Kohath comes after. The most dangerous job is the privileged one. God's protection is sometimes a curtain that hides what the eye cannot survive.

Gershon — from thirty to fifty. Service: bear the curtains, the tent, the covering, badgers' skins above, the door hanging, court hangings, gate hanging, cords, instruments. By Ithamar son of Aaron's oversight. Guidance: Gershon carries the soft pieces — fabric, ropes, leather coverings. The whole roof and walls of God's house. They don't see the inside; they protect what's inside from rain and dust. Hidden work, holy work.

Merari — from thirty to fifty. Service: bear the boards of the tabernacle, bars, pillars, sockets, pillars of the court round about, sockets, pins, cords, instruments. By Ithamar's oversight. Guidance: Merari carries the heavy frame — boards, sockets, pins. Their work is the load no one wants. Even God's house needs the bones; even the bones get weighed and counted.

Numbered: Kohath 30-50 — 2,750. Gershon 30-50 — 2,630. Merari 30-50 — 3,200. Total: 8,580. As the LORD commanded Moses, every man to his service and burden. Guidance: 8,580 men of working age inside the smallest tribe. Each one knew his bag, his board, his bundle. When Israel struck camp, the holy things were packed by name. Holiness needs project management.
